title: oxalis hochreutineri
intro: Oxalis hochreutineri, also known as the Hochreutiner wood sorrel, is a species of flowering plant in the family Oxalidaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its high levels of oxalic acid, a compound with antiseptic and antibacterial properties. Oxalis hochreutineri has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, treating wounds, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. The plant is typically used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, where it can be consumed as a dietary supplement or applied topically to the skin.
